name: Grok Build 0.1
archDisplay: Architecture undisclosed; a dedicated, fast agentic-coding model (100+ tok/s) purpose-built to power the Grok Build CLI, distinct from the general-purpose Grok line
note: Confirmed real and distinct: xAI's (SpaceXAI's) first model purpose-built for agentic coding, powering the Grok Build CLI/terminal agent. Opened to developers via the API in public beta on ~May 29, 2026, a bit after the underlying model went live (~May 20, 2026). 256K context, text+image input, always-on reasoning, function calling, structured outputs, native MCP ('bring your own MCP') support. Not to be confused with 'Grok Build' the product/CLI/agent name, which Grok 4.5 now also powers as of July 2026 — Grok Build 0.1 was the original dedicated coding model before Grok 4.5 became the CLI's default.

results.notes: Terminal-Bench v2.1 (agentic coding & terminal use), from Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Evaluations breakdown for the 'Grok Build 0.1 0616' checkpoint. Directly fetched/rendered the live AA model page and read off the model's position in the Terminal-Bench v2.1 ranked list (52%, 28th of 29 models shown).
GPQA Diamond, from Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Evaluations breakdown for 'Grok Build 0.1 0616' (89%, 23rd of 29 models shown).
Humanity's Last Exam, from Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Evaluations breakdown for 'Grok Build 0.1 0616' (36%, 23rd of 29 models shown).

Artificial Analysis's live model page (checkpoint '0616', matching this model's 256K ctx / $1-$2 pricing) lists a 11% τ³-Banking score. [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]
Bonus/out-of-scope addition beyond the 3 assigned gaps; flagging clearly in case the orchestrator wants to review scope before merging.

verificationNotes:
Existence confirmed via SpaceXAI's own docs page (docs.x.ai/developers/models/grok-build-0.1) and multiple independent write-ups (DevOps.com, Kilo Code blog, OpenRouter, Vercel AI Gateway, Kingy.ai) all describing the same model with matching specs: 256K context, $1/$2 per Mtok pricing, text+image input. Release-date sources disagree in the details — different write-ups cite May 14, May 20, May 27, and May 29, 2026 for various milestones (underlying model live vs. subscriber CLI rollout vs. public API beta); I used May 20, 2026 as the best-supported date for the model itself going live, per Kilo Code's blog and a cross-source search, but flagged this as approximate. VENDOR-REPORTED SWE-BENCH: I could NOT find a credible, corroborated vendor benchmark score. One set of aggregator sites (Pickurai, Superconductor, BenchLM) mentioned a ~70.8% SWE-Bench Verified figure, but two independent direct-fetch sources (DevOps.com and Kingy.ai) explicitly state the model 'enters without published benchmarks.' Given this contradiction, still omitted. WIDER SEARCH (this round, not repeating the above dead end): not a Chinese or Japanese vendor (xAI/SpaceXAI), so no CN/JP-language sources apply. Directly browser-rendered the live Artificial Analysis model page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/grok-build-0-1-06-16 — the '0616' AA checkpoint matches this model's specs exactly: 256K ctx, $1/$2 per Mtok) and read off its per-benchmark rank position in AA's Intelligence Evaluations breakdown, yielding 3 taxonomy-eligible results: Terminal-Bench v2.1 (52%), GPQA Diamond (89%), and Humanity's Last Exam (36%). AA's page also lists GDPval-AA v2 (36% normalized index) and 𝜏³-Banking (11%) for this model, but deliberately did NOT map these: GDPval-AA v2 is displayed as an '(Elo-500)/2000' normalized percentage while this corpus's existing gdpval-aa entries store the raw Elo, so mapping the percentage would conflate two different score scales; 𝜏³-Banking has no matching taxonomy slug (tau-bench is domain-generic upstream τ-bench, tau2-bench-telecom is telecom-specific — Banking domain doesn't cleanly fit either without guessing). Also found real, citable evidence of a separate LMArena/Arena.ai 'Agent Arena' leaderboard placement — official @arena account (x.com/arena/status/2064084859103166518) states 'Grok Build 0.1 ranks #15... in the new Agent Arena leaderboard' (added 2026-06-08 per arena.ai/blog/leaderboard-changelog) — but did not report it: this is a rank on a distinct agentic-behavior leaderboard (Elo-free, based on task-completion/steerability signals), not the Elo-scored LMArena Text leaderboard this corpus's 'arena' slug represents elsewhere (e.g. ernie-5-0-preview.json's arena entry is an Elo score), so force-mapping a bare rank number into that slug's Elo-scored field would misrepresent it. Checked OpenRouter model page directly (no benchmark scores shown, pricing/routing info only) and searched LMArena Text/Code leaderboards, Wayback Machine (no dedicated launch-page snapshot found beyond the still-live docs.x.ai page), and general web search — no other independently-verifiable, taxonomy-mappable benchmark numbers found.

2026-07-16: Researched the 7 missing core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu); all resolved as omitted_no_evidence — xAI never published classic academic benchmarks for this specialized coding-CLI model. OFFICIAL: re-fetched docs.x.ai/developers/models/grok-build-0.1 directly — page has zero benchmark numbers of any kind (specs/pricing only); x.ai/news/grok-build-0-1 returns HTTP 403 to direct fetch, and search-snippet coverage of it likewise shows no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench/MMMU figures. INDEPENDENT LEADERBOARDS: Artificial Analysis's live model page (checkpoint '0616') was re-rendered in full — its Intelligence Index v4.1 (9 evals) plus every 'Additional Evaluation' listed (AA-Briefcase, AutomationBench-AA, Harvey LAB-AA, EnterpriseOps-Gym-AA, IFBench, APEX-Agents-AA, ITBench-AA, MMMU-Pro) contain none of the 7 target slugs; MMMU-Pro is tracked by AA but its 10-model table (Gemini 3.5 Flash down to Llama 4 Scout) does not include Grok Build 0.1 at all. AA's dedicated LiveCodeBench leaderboard (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/livecodebench) and Coding-Agents index (artificialanalysis.ai/agents/coding-agents, built from DeepSWE/Terminal-Bench v2/SWE-Atlas-QnA) also do not list this model. Vals.ai has a dedicated page (vals.ai/models/grok_grok-build-0.1) with exactly one benchmark for this model, 'Vibe Code Bench v1.1' at 0.0% ± 3.82 (rank 53/72) — not one of the 7 target slugs and not usable. Epoch AI has zero coverage of Grok Build 0.1 anywhere on epoch.ai (confirmed via both search and a direct fetch of epoch.ai/benchmarks). CROSS-REFERENCE / mislabeling caught: several low-quality aggregators (BenchLM.ai, Pickurai, Superconductor, benchable.ai, aibenchy.com) repeat a '70.8% SWE-Bench Verified' figure for 'the model powering Grok Build.' Traced this to its actual source: it is grok-code-fast-1's score (confirmed via Hacker News discussion of xAI's own grok-code-fast-1 announcement and x.ai/news/grok-code-fast-1), a distinct, earlier (Sept 2025) xAI coding model — not Grok Build 0.1 (May 2026). This corroborates, with a concrete mechanism, the existing verificationNotes' finding that DevOps.com/Kingy.ai say the model 'enters without published benchmarks.' Also caught BenchLM.ai fabricating a '76.5% MMMU-Pro, sourced to Artificial Analysis' figure for this model — false, per direct verification of AA's live MMMU-Pro table above; treating BenchLM as unreliable for this model generally (it self-discloses only 15 of 313 tracked benchmarks are non-generated/published, and excludes this model from its own public leaderboard for insufficient real coverage). No CN/JP/regional leaderboard applies (xAI/SpaceXAI is a US lab). Conclusion: a real, expected absence for a narrow agentic-coding model whose vendor deliberately did not run legacy academic suites — not a research gap.

2026-07-17 (round-2 deep gap chase, mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, NEW source types only — no repeat of official launch page/system card/Epoch AI/Vals.ai/LMArena/BenchLM-leaderboard-rank checks from the prior round): Checked all 6 prescribed new source types. (1) Stanford HELM — went beyond page-rendering (SPA content isn't visible to fetch tools) by pulling HELM's own data files directly: HELM Lite's live 'latest' release is v1.13.0, whose own summary.json is dated 2025-01-10, and HELM Classic's 'latest' is v0.4.0, dated 2023-11-17 — both releases predate Grok Build 0.1's existence (May 2026) entirely. Downloaded each release's full runs.json (117MB for Lite, 1.6GB for Classic — the complete list of every model x scenario run HELM has ever published under these releases) and grepped case-insensitively for 'grok' and 'xai': zero matches in either file. HELM has never evaluated any xAI/Grok model at all under its current public releases, let alone this one — a clean, direct-evidence rule-out, not just an absence-of-search-results. (2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar — site:arxiv.org search for the exact model name returned only unrelated 'grokking' papers; general Semantic Scholar search returned no dedicated entry or citing paper. No academic paper found using Grok Build 0.1 as a baseline. (3) GitHub eval-harness run logs — searched for lm-evaluation-harness/openbench/results.json runs against 'grok-build-0.1' or 'x-ai/grok-build-0.1'; only found xai-org/grok-build (the open-sourced *coding-agent harness/TUI* itself, published 2026-07-15) and a fan fork — neither is an eval-harness results log. No committed benchmark-run artifact found anywhere on GitHub. (4) Other leaderboards/aggregators — LiveBench.ai's homepage is a client-rendered SPA with no server-side content (confirmed via direct curl, 1KB shell) and no search hits reference it for this model; Scale Labs/SEAL leaderboard has no listing for this model; PapersWithCode's search now 302-redirects to huggingface.co/papers (the standalone PwC leaderboards are defunct) — no hit either way; OpenRouter's dedicated /performance sub-page (distinct URL from the base page already checked in round 1) was re-checked and likewise shows only latency/throughput/pricing panels, no benchmark scores. BenchLM.ai's full profile page was re-fetched directly (not just search snippets) and explicitly enumerates its 15 published/non-generated benchmarks for this model by name (AA Agentic Index, AA Coding Index, AA GPQA Diamond, AA Humanity's Last Exam, AA MMMU-Pro, AA Omniscience family, GDPval-AA, AA SciCode, AA Long Context Reasoning, Critical Physics Tasks, Gert Labs Composite) — MMLU, MATH, AIME, and HumanEval are confirmed absent from that explicit list, corroborating rather than contradicting the prior round's BenchLM-fabrication finding. (5) Non-English sources — Japanese tech blogs (innovatopia.jp, ai-revolution.co.jp, uravation.com) covering the Grok Build 0.1 launch were found and reviewed; they discuss pricing/context/positioning vs. Claude Code and Codex but report no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figures, and one explicitly states the model's benchmark scores 'have not yet been officially announced' and warns against reusing grok-code-fast-1's older SWE-bench number for it. Korean- and Chinese-language searches returned no on-topic results at all (no regional blog appears to have independently tested this specific model). (6) Sibling models — found and fetched a direct Grok 4.5-vs-Grok-Build-0.1 comparison table (opencode.ai/data/compare/xai/grok-4-5/xai/grok-build-0-1); it only reports normalized 0-100 composite indices (Reasoning 100, Coding n/a, Cost-efficiency 54, Context-window 52, Multimodal 60, Tool-use n/a) rather than raw academic-benchmark percentages, so it is not usable as a citable MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for either model. No other sibling-model release material (Grok 4.5 launch page/system card) was found citing Grok Build 0.1's score on these 4 benchmarks. Conclusion: all 6 new source types were genuinely checked (exceeding the 3-type minimum), all came back empty or explicitly confirmed-absent for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval specifically. This reinforces, via entirely independent evidence paths, the prior round's 'omitted_no_evidence' conclusion: Grok Build 0.1 is a narrow agentic-coding model that no vendor, academic, or third-party evaluator (that could be located) has run through the classic MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval suites. No corpus changes made.

2026-07-18 (round-3 targeted gap chase: livecodebench / swe-bench / mmmu, fresh live-browser verification beyond the 2026-07-16/17 rounds' WebFetch-only passes): Modality re-check first — this model's own corpus record lists modalities:[text,vision] and capabilities.vision:true, so MMMU is architecturally APPLICABLE for this model (not inapplicable); any absence must be treated as an evidence gap, not assumed.

LIVECODEBENCH: (1) Re-fetched docs.x.ai/developers/models/grok-build-0.1 — still specs-only, zero benchmark numbers. (2) Live-browser-rendered (full DOM read_page/get_page_text, not just WebFetch-summarized) the model's own Artificial Analysis page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/grok-build-0-1-06-16) end-to-end: confirmed LiveCodeBench is absent from all 18-of-22 AA evaluations shown for this model (9 Intelligence Index components + 8 Additional Evaluations + MMMU-Pro). (3) Live-rendered AA's dedicated LiveCodeBench Benchmark Leaderboard (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/livecodebench) directly: it tracks only "7 of 343 models" system-wide (Gemini 3 Pro Preview, Gemini 3 Flash, DeepSeek V3.2 Speciale, GPT-5.2, GLM-4.7, gpt-oss-120b, Claude 4.5 Haiku) — Grok Build 0.1 is not among them; AA has simply never run this eval for it. (4) Live-rendered the official livecodebench.github.io/leaderboard.html directly (the prior round's WebFetch attempt only got a "Loading..." placeholder): its full 28-model ranked table (O4-Mini down to Claude-3-Haiku) is entirely pre-2026 models, and its available date-range picker tops out at 5/1/2025 — the official site has not yet incorporated any 2026-era model at all, so Grok Build 0.1 (released May 2026) categorically cannot appear there yet; this is a source-side gap, not a search failure. (5) BenchLM.ai's full model profile (freshly re-fetched) explicitly enumerates all 15 of its published/non-generated benchmarks by name for this model — LiveCodeBench is not among them. Conclusion: omitted_no_evidence stands, now corroborated by direct live-rendered checks of both official trackers (not just search snippets).

SWE-BENCH: (1) Re-fetched official docs — no figure. (2) Live-rendered AA's model page and cross-referenced against AA's full "Explore Evaluations" index (26 named evaluations enumerated on the LiveCodeBench leaderboard page: Intelligence Index, GDPval-AA v2, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, APEX-Agents-AA, AutomationBench-AA, Harvey LAB-AA, EnterpriseOps-Gym-AA, τ³-Banking, ITBench-AA, MMMU-Pro, IFBench, Terminal-Bench Hard, τ²-Bench Telecom, MMLU-Pro, LiveCodeBench, MATH-500, AIME 2025, Global-MMLU-Lite, Openness Index) — SWE-bench is not one of AA's tracked evaluations at all, for any model; AA substitutes its own Terminal-Bench v2.1 for agentic-coding measurement. This is a structural rule-out, stronger than "not found for this model." (3) Live-rendered the official swebench.com leaderboard directly (prior WebFetch attempt only returned a placeholder): its "Verified" board, dated 2026-02-17 to 2026-02-26 (genuinely current, unlike the LiveCodeBench site), lists 14 models — Claude 4.5 Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, Gemini 3 Flash/Pro, MiniMax M2.5, Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5-2 (+Codex variants), GLM-5, Kimi K2.5, DeepSeek V3.2, GPT-5 Mini — and contains zero Grok or xAI entries of any kind. This is a clean, direct, current rule-out: xAI does not appear on the official SWE-bench Verified leaderboard under any model name as of its latest update. (4) Re-verified the widely-repeated "70.8%" aggregator figure: direct fetch of its origin, Superconductor's blog post (superconductor.com/blog/grok-build-benchmark), shows it actually concerns a custom in-house "Personal SWE-Bench" built from each customer's own real merged PRs (not the official 500-instance SWE-bench Verified set), and the post gives no explicit numeric score for Grok Build 0.1 at all — reinforcing the prior round's finding that the 70.8% figure circulating on aggregators actually belongs to grok-code-fast-1, a distinct, earlier xAI model. (5) BenchLM's explicit 15-benchmark list for this model excludes SWE-bench. Conclusion: omitted_no_evidence stands, now backed by a direct, current (Feb-2026) official-leaderboard rule-out.

MMMU: Confirmed vision capability is real (see modality re-check above), so this is a genuine research question, not inapplicability. Live-rendered AA's model page and found this model's MMMU-Pro (harder, vision-only-input variant — a separate taxonomy slug, mmmu-pro, from plain 'mmmu' per corpus/benchmarks.json) score is now present: 77%, in a 21-model ranked table. This is new since 2026-07-16, when the prior round found the model absent from AA's MMMU-Pro table entirely — AA appears to have incrementally back-filled this evaluation. Cross-validated the list-position-reading method by re-deriving this model's four already-corpus-confirmed scores from the same live page using identical logic (GPQA Diamond 89%, HLE 36%, Terminal-Bench v2.1 52%, τ³-Banking 11%) — all four matched the existing corpus values exactly, confirming the reading is reliable, not a misattribution. However, AA does not track plain MMMU as a separate evaluation at all (only MMMU-Pro and MMLU-Pro appear anywhere in AA's full 26-evaluation index) — so AA cannot be a source for plain MMMU for any model, this one included. BenchLM's explicit 15-benchmark list has no MMMU entry either. WebSearch queries for "Grok Build 0.1 MMMU" returned only the (distinct) MMMU-Pro figure, sometimes loosely labeled "MMMU" by search-summarization but not by any primary source. No plain-MMMU score exists for this model anywhere checked. Conclusion: 'mmmu' remains omitted_no_evidence — a genuine absence (AA's evaluation suite has simply substituted MMMU-Pro for plain MMMU industry-wide, and no other tracker fills the gap), not a search failure, and not architectural inapplicability. The newly-confirmed MMMU-Pro=77% figure is proposed separately below as new data for the distinct mmmu-pro slug (previously entirely missing for this model), since conflating it into 'mmmu' would misrepresent a harder, different benchmark as the base one.

Net outcome of this round: livecodebench, swe-bench, and mmmu all remain omitted_no_evidence, each now backed by stronger and more current corroborating evidence (including two official-leaderboard direct rule-outs) than the 2026-07-16 round had. One incidental, out-of-scope-but-verified addition made: mmmu-pro = 77 (independent, Artificial Analysis, live-rendered 2026-07-18).
